document_name: Proof of household income for the last 30 days
citation_note: Application asks for proof of income received in the last 30 days, such as pay stubs, award letters, or pay checks.

document_name: Social Security numbers for household members applying for SNAP
citation_note: Federal law requires SNAP applicants to provide SSNs or apply for one.

document_name: Immigration / alien documentation for non-U.S. citizen applicants
citation_note: The application requests U.S. citizen status and says alien documentation is required if the answer is no.

document_name: Proof of household expenses if claiming deductions
citation_note: Application notes the household may need proof of expenses and that deductions will not be applied if expenses are not reported and verified when required.

document_name: Signed SNAP application or COMPASS submission confirmation
citation_note: SNAP can be applied for online, in person, or by mail; a submitted application is required to get an official determination.

document_name: Social Security numbers or document numbers for legal immigrants for everyone needing insurance
citation_note: The health coverage application lists SSNs or immigration document numbers as needed information.

document_name: Employer and income information for everyone in the household (pay stubs, W-2s, wage/tax statements)
citation_note: The health coverage application lists employer and income information as needed information.

document_name: Policy numbers for current or recent past health insurance
citation_note: The application lists policy numbers for any current or recent past health insurance.

document_name: Information about any job-related health insurance available to the family
citation_note: The application lists job-related health insurance information as needed information.
General eligibility page states identity can be verified by a driver license, state ID, or another piece of identification.

document_name: Proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful non-citizen status
citation_note: General eligibility page says U.S. citizens, refugees, and lawfully admitted non-citizens must submit documentation.
Some non-MAGI eligibility groups have resource limits; collect only when the case may fall into an older-adult, disability, LTC, HCBS, or MAWD pathway.

document_name: Recent bill from the main heating source
citation_note: LIHEAP page says applicants must include a recent bill from the main heating source.

document_name: Proof of household income for each household member
citation_note: LIHEAP page says applicants must include proof of income for each household member.

document_name: Employer statements, pay stubs, or award letters for income verification
citation_note: LIHEAP page gives these as examples of acceptable income verification.

document_name: Documentation of lawful status if applicant is not a U.S. citizen
citation_note: LIHEAP page says additional documents may be required if the applicant is not a U.S. citizen.

document_name: Lease or rent documentation if heating bill is included in rent
citation_note: LIHEAP page says additional documents may be required if the heating bill is included in rent.

document_name: Additional financial documentation if income is less than monthly basic living needs
citation_note: LIHEAP page says additional documents may be required if income is less than the cost of monthly basic living needs.
